title: Sour Dense Korean Tomato Dumplings
description: A show‑off Korean dumpling featuring a tangy tomato reduction, chewy cassava‑based wrapper, and a savory pork filling for the Whole30.
instructions: In a large bowl, combine ground pork, diced onion, minced garlic, grated ginger, chopped green onion, fish sauce, apple cider vinegar, and gochugaru. Mix well and set aside.

Heat 1 tablespoon coconut oil in a saucepan over medium‑high heat and pour in the tomato juice. Bring to a gentle simmer, reducing the liquid by half until it thickens slightly, about 5 minutes. Remove from heat and keep warm.

In a separate mixing bowl, whisk cassava flour with a pinch of sea salt. Add the melted coconut oil and water gradually, stirring until a soft, pliable dough forms. Knead lightly until smooth and let the dough rest for 5 minutes.
Divide the rested dough into 12 equal portions. Roll each piece into a small ball, then flatten into a 3‑inch diameter disc. Use a cookie cutter or a glass to cut out 4‑inch circles of dough.

Place about 1 teaspoon of the pork filling onto the center of each dough circle. Fold the dough over the filling to create a crescent shape and press the edges together, sealing them with a pinch of water if needed.

Fill a large pot with 8 cups of water and bring to a rolling boil. Gently slide the dumplings into the water, stirring briefly to prevent sticking. Cook until the dumplings rise to the surface, then simmer for an additional 4–5 minutes.
Meanwhile, ladle the warm tomato reduction into individual bowls. Drop the cooked dumplings into the sauce, allowing them to soak for a minute.
Garnish each serving with extra chopped green onion, a drizzle of sesame oil (optional), and a light dusting of gochugaru for color.
Serve the dumplings hot as a hearty, sour, dense Korean entrée.
